title: rosa agrestis subsp. pubescens
intro: Rosa agrestis subsp. pubescens, also known as the wild rose or Rosa rugosa, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family, characterized by its hairy leaves and stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rosa agrestis subsp. pubescens include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
